
Two notes on judgment calls worth surfacing. First, I emitted no `definitions` — the statute names "quality, objectivity, utility, and integrity" but never defines those terms, deferring definition to the OMB guidelines it mandates. Emitting a Definition would require inventing text the document does not contain. Second, the four attributes are modeled as `has_component` of an information-quality Concept rather than as Constructs, per the instruction that promotion to measurability is an operator decision.

The document's dominant structure — statutory duty, deadline, trigger, and reporting relation — is largely outside the current edge vocabulary, so eight relationships went to `proposed_relationships` rather than being forced into `precedes` or `has_component`. If this corpus is mostly statutes, that set is a reasonable starting point for a legal-obligation edge extension.
